# OpenPlan3D

> OpenPlan3D is a free, open-source floor plan software that runs in the browser — an alternative to RoomSketcher, Floorplanner, and SketchUp for 2D floor plans and 3D home design, with no subscription, watermarks, or account.

## Summary
- OpenPlan3D is a free, open-source floor plan editor that runs entirely in your web browser.
- It is 100% free: no premium tier, no watermarks, no paywalls, and no signup or account.
- You can draw 2D floor plans with walls, rooms, doors, and windows at precise imperial or metric measurements.
- You can switch to a real-time 3D view and orbit through the design at any time.
- You can furnish plans with 140+ built-in furniture models or import your own GLB/glTF 3D meshes.
- You can scan a real room with an iPhone using the free OpenPlan3D Capture app (iPhone LiDAR + Apple RoomPlan) and open the scan directly in the editor.
- You can export floor plans to PNG and PDF for sharing, SVG for vector graphics, DXF for AutoCAD and other CAD software, and JSON for re-editing.
- Your plans stay on your device — the editor runs locally in the browser with no cloud requirement.
- The full source code is open source on GitHub.
- Common use cases: real estate listings, remodels and renovations, interior design, moving and furniture planning, landlord and rental documentation, and CAD workflows.

## Free alternative to paid apps
- OpenPlan3D is a free, open-source alternative to paid floor plan apps including magicplan, RoomSketcher, Floorplanner, Planner 5D, and SketchUp.
- Unlike those apps, OpenPlan3D has no subscription, no watermarks on exports, and requires no account.
- It offers free DXF/CAD export and free iPhone LiDAR room scanning (via the OpenPlan3D Capture app), features that competitors typically gate behind paid plans.
- It is also a free alternative people compare against other floor plan and home design tools, including Cedreo, Coohom, HomeByMe, Homestyler, SmartDraw, Live Home 3D, Chief Architect, Room Planner, Foyr Neo, Sweet Home 3D, Space Designer 3D, Roomstyler, Morpholio Board, and AutoCAD.
- Comparison details: https://openplan3d.com/alternatives

## OpenPlan3D Capture (iOS app)
- Free, open-source iPhone/iPad app that scans rooms with LiDAR and Apple RoomPlan to create editable floor plans.
- Detects walls, doors, windows, and furniture automatically; fine-tune on the phone and view in 3D.
- Open a scan directly in the OpenPlan3D web editor, or export to PNG, SVG, DXF, PDF, or USDZ.
- No account required; scans stay on the device. Status: coming soon to the App Store.
- Details: https://openplan3d.com/capture

## Related tool: 3D Splat App
- 3D Splat App turns photos and videos into photorealistic 3D scenes with Gaussian Splatting on your Mac — no LiDAR required.
- From the makers of OpenPlan3D. Useful for capturing a real space before designing its floor plan.
- Details: https://3dsplatapp.com/

## Links
- Website: https://openplan3d.com
- Editor: https://app.openplan3d.com
- iPhone app: https://openplan3d.com/capture
- GitHub: https://github.com/theLodgeBots/open3dFloorplan
- 3D Splat App (Gaussian Splatting on Mac): https://3dsplatapp.com/
